title: Non GATE CSE - Object Oriented Programming - UGC NET CSE  December 2012  Part 2  Question

sections.name: UGC NET CSE  December 2012  Part 2  Question

questions.question:
Match the following with respect to \( \text{C++} \) data types :

\( \begin{array}{clcl}  \text{a.} & \text{User defined type} & \text{i.} & \text{Qualifier} \\ \text{b.} & \text{Built in type} & \text{ii.} & \text{Union} \\ \text{c.} & \text{Derived Type} & \text{iii.} & \text{Void} \\  \text{d.} & \text{Long double} & \text{iv.} & \text{Pointer} \\ \end{array} \)

\( \textbf{Code :} \)



Object Oriented Programming: UGC NET CSE | December 2012 | Part 2 | Question: 39

Polymorphism means :



Object Oriented Programming: UGC NET CSE | December 2004 | Part 2 | Question: 15
options: Runtime type identification within a class hierarchy

questions.question:
Which of the following is true of constructor function in C++ ?



Object Oriented Programming: UGC NET CSE | December 2007 | Part 2 | Question: 15
options: A class must have at least one constructor.
A constructor is a unique function which cannot be overloaded.
A constructor function must be invoked with the object name.
A constructor function is automatically invoked when an object is created.

questions.question:
Which of the following is not true in C++ ?

 



Object Oriented Programming: UGC NET CSE | December 2007 | Part 2 | Question: 14
options: “Private” elements of a base class are not accessible by members of its derived class.
“Protected” elements of base class are not accessible by members of its derived class.
When base class access specified is “Public”, public elements of the base class become public members of its derived class.
When base class access specified is “Public”, protected elements of a base class become protected members of its derived class.
